method.note_on_attempts_jsonl_args_redaction: WorldKernel._denied() logs only the small 'args' dict its caller passes in, not the full call arguments. For submit_application this means every DENIED attempt's logged args show only application_id, regardless of what customer_id/product were actually supplied; only the denial_reason text (e.g. 'missing required fields: customer_id, product') gives direct proof of emptiness for a given call. The SUCCESS path logs the full payload including customer_id and product. This analysis relies on denial_reason text, not redacted args, wherever it asserts a field was empty.

submit_application_attempts_all_seats.denied_reason: basis.retrieved must be a non-empty list

evidence.consent: 顧客が手続き希望を表明し、給与明書による本人確認手続きの同意を得た。希望期限4/16。

submit_application_attempts_all_seats.denied_reason: basis citation_handle was not read before action: DFH-SAL-024
missing completion evidence: consent_log_id, material_version, recording_id

record_customer_contact_customer_id_fiel.note: customer_id argument value observed in attempts.jsonl for tool record_customer_contact (both success and denial paths preserve this field; see method note). Common 'other' values include agent-fabricated strings such as CUST-APP-R25, CUST-APP-P-04, C-R13, and literal placeholders 'unknown' / uncertain-kanji / 'CUST-????'.

primary_causes: customer_id_never_rendered_to_any_seat_prompt: _render_inbox_message (harness.py) renders the customer_utterance inbox line as an application-id-and-product business record for the sales seat and never includes customer_id, even though customer_id is present in the same message payload; the chat-kind branch renders only sender/channel/free-text body. No seat, sales included, ever sees a literal customer_id string in its natural-language prompt.

no_lookup_tool_exists: grep of src/company_twin/tools.py and kernel.py shows no tool that resolves customer_id from application_id or queries any customer registry/CRM; ROLE_TOOL_BUNDLES gives both the sales role and the application role the submit_application tool, but neither role has a way to look up a real customer_id it was not already handed.

handoff_chat_is_free_text_only: chat-kind inbox messages are rendered from body text with no structured fields; across 149 successful send_chat deliveries to emp-C in the 4 runs, 0 contain a CUS-prefixed id (customer_id), while 145 (97.3 percent) contain an application id and 94 (63.1 percent) name a product.

consequence_observed_at_the_tool_call: the r4_contradict (trial D) single submit_application attempt by emp-C was denied with the kernel's own reason string 'missing required fields: customer_id, product', which is direct proof (via WorldKernel.submit_application's own required-field check) that both fields were empty at that call; the r1_control (trial A) sole success was placed by the sales seat that owned the customer event (emp-B, primary_seat of APP-R18) submitting directly rather than via emp-C, and even then the customer_id value it supplied was 'APP-R18' (the application id, not the real customer id 'CUS-R18') because submit_application performs no existence/correctness check on customer_id, only a non-empty check.
excluded_causes: control_gate_denial: all five K-* knobs are False in config.json for all 4 runs at run start; the only control gate that ever fired against submit_application in these runs is K-completion-gate, which the world profile itself switches on mid-run at tick 30 in all 4 runs (kernel.py fire_timed_events, scc_switch) -- arm-symmetric and seed-independent, confirmed by the completion_gate_active ledger event appearing at tick 30 in all four runs; it is not a K-* denial of submit_application's core required fields.

handoff_address_resolution_failure: this was the pilot's (2026-07-10) primary cause and is now closed; approval-#14's contact directory produced 26/39/38/46 successful sends to emp-C per trial (149 total), a large improvement over the prior pilot's 0/4.
downstream_turn_starvation: this was the pilot's secondary cause and is now closed; emp-C received 14/22/16/16 turns per trial (68 total) versus 5 turns total across the whole 2026-07-10 pilot.

model_specific_failure: the same pattern (customer_id present in 0/149 handoff chats; only application_id and sometimes product carried) recurs across two seeds (953, 954), two probe families (R1, R4), and both control and treatment arms.
mutation_side_effect: the pattern is identical in both control arms (A, C), which carry no document mutation.
diagnosis.mechanism: customer_id is generated and stored in the kernel's internal event/application state (customer_event, application_drafted payloads) but is dropped by the natural-language rendering layer for every seat: the sales seat's own inbox line for a new case shows only the application id and product, never customer_id, and the chat rendering used for sales-to-application handoff is unstructured free text. As a result sales itself frequently does not have a literal customer_id to write into a handoff chat (directly evidenced: multiple sales-authored chat bodies to emp-C literally ask emp-C to supply or confirm the customer ID), and no tool anywhere in the tool bundle can resolve one from an application_id. The application-role seat (emp-C) attempted submit_application 7 times across the 4 runs (6 in trial C, 1 in trial D) and never attempted request_approval/verify_identity/link_review either before or after. The one clean, directly-provable denial (trial D) cites missing customer_id and product by the kernel's own required-field check. The lone success (trial A) occurred only because the sales seat that generated the case bypassed the handoff and submitted directly using its own (already fabricated, non-validated) placeholder customer_id -- submit_application performs no existence check, only a non-empty check, on customer_id/application_id.
